examples.title: Pseudocode: Mutual Exclusion
examples.description: Binary semaphore ensuring only one process can be in its critical section at a time
examples.explanation: This pseudocode demonstrates how a binary semaphore (mutex) can be used to ensure mutual exclusion between multiple processes.

examples.title: Python: Producer-Consumer Problem
examples.description: Counting semaphores managing a fixed-size buffer
examples.code:
import threading
import time

# Buffer size
BUFFER_SIZE = 5

# Semaphores
mutex = threading.Semaphore(1)
empty = threading.Semaphore(BUFFER_SIZE)
full = threading.Semaphore(0)

# Buffer
buffer = [None] * BUFFER_SIZE

# Producer function
def producer(item):
 empty.acquire()
 mutex.acquire()
 # Add item to buffer
 for i in range(BUFFER_SIZE):
 if buffer[i] is None:
 buffer[i] = item
 break
 mutex.release()
 full.release()

# Consumer function
def consumer():
 full.acquire()
 mutex.acquire()
 # Remove item from buffer
 for i in range(BUFFER_SIZE):
 if buffer[i] is not None:
 item = buffer[i]
 buffer[i] = None
 break
 mutex.release()
 empty.release()
 return item

# Create threads
producer_thread = threading.Thread(target=producer, args=('item',))
consumer_thread = threading.Thread(target=consumer)

# Start threads
producer_thread.start()
consumer_thread.start()

# Wait for threads to finish
producer_thread.join()
consumer_thread.join()
examples.explanation: This Python code demonstrates how counting semaphores can be used to manage a fixed-size buffer in a producer-consumer scenario.
